### Calls per Active User

Weekly call centre volume divided by the size of the active user base, so you can
compare support demand across weeks even as the user base grows.

Raw call volume moves partly because the number of people using CanadaLogin
changes. Dividing calls by daily active users takes that out and leaves how often
a typical user needed to phone in. The figure is scaled to calls per 1,000 daily
active users to keep it in a readable range.

*Alternative names*:

- call centre contact rate
- normalized call volume
- support contacts per active user
- calls per 1,000 users

**Calculation.** For each call centre reporting week, take `calls_accepted` from
`call_centre.weekly_activity_report` and divide it by the average daily active
users over that week, then multiply by 1,000. Daily active users come from
`ibm_verify.auth_total_logins.unique_users`; the weekly average is the mean of
that field over the days whose `date` falls inside the week's
`date_range_start` to `date_range_end`. The two tables store their dates as text,
so both sides of the join are cast to `DATE`.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## SQL

```sql
SELECT
    w.date_range_start,
    w.date_range_end,
    CAST(w.calls_accepted AS BIGINT) AS calls_accepted,
    AVG(a.unique_users) AS avg_daily_active_users,
    CAST(w.calls_accepted AS DOUBLE) / AVG(a.unique_users) * 1000
        AS calls_per_1000_dau
FROM call_centre.weekly_activity_report AS w
JOIN ibm_verify.auth_total_logins AS a
    ON CAST(a.date AS DATE)
       BETWEEN CAST(w.date_range_start AS DATE) AND CAST(w.date_range_end AS DATE)
GROUP BY w.date_range_start, w.date_range_end, w.calls_accepted
ORDER BY w.date_range_start;
```

## R

```r
weekly <- tbl(con, in_schema("call_centre", "weekly_activity_report")) |>
  select(date_range_start, date_range_end, calls_accepted)

dau <- tbl(con, in_schema("ibm_verify", "auth_total_logins")) |>
  select(dau_date = date, unique_users)

weekly |>
  inner_join(dau, by = character()) |>
  filter(
    as.Date(dau_date) >= as.Date(date_range_start),
    as.Date(dau_date) <= as.Date(date_range_end)
  ) |>
  group_by(date_range_start, date_range_end, calls_accepted) |>
  summarise(
    avg_daily_active_users = mean(unique_users, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    calls_per_1000_dau = calls_accepted / avg_daily_active_users * 1000
  ) |>
  arrange(date_range_start) |>
  select(
    date_range_start,
    date_range_end,
    calls_accepted,
    avg_daily_active_users,
    calls_per_1000_dau
  )
```

:::

*Interpretation notes.* The denominator is the average daily active users over the
week, not distinct users across the whole week, so it measures typical daily reach
rather than total weekly reach. A week only appears once daily active user data
exists for it: `auth_total_logins` begins near the April 22, 2026 public launch,
and the join drops any earlier call centre week that has no matching days. The
numerator is `calls_accepted`, which includes test calls, so weeks with unusual
test activity read slightly high.
